Greenfield Farmers Market Events

Greenfield Farmers Market hosts a range of community events throughout the season, from free cooking demonstrations to our beloved annual festivals. All events take place at Greenfield Town Square unless otherwise noted. Most events are free to attend.

Weekly Events

Saturday Morning Cooking Demonstrations

Every Saturday at 10am, a local chef or nutritionist leads a free thirty-minute cooking demonstration using ingredients available fresh from market vendors that morning. Past themes have included farm-to-table summer salads, fermented foods for gut health, sheet pan roasting for autumn vegetables, and whole-grain baking with heritage flours. Demonstrations are held at the market demonstration kitchen near the information booth.

Kids Corner

Every Saturday from 9am to 12pm, the Kids Corner offers free hands-on activities for children aged three to twelve. Activities include vegetable planting, simple cooking projects, farm animal interaction, and seasonal craft projects. All activities are free and run by trained market volunteers. Parental supervision is required for children under six years of age.

Seasonal Festivals

Spring Opening Day

The market opens for the season on the first Saturday of April with a special Opening Day celebration. This annual event features expanded vendor participation, live music from local bluegrass ensemble Clover Creek, complimentary coffee and pastries from Heartland Bakehouse, and a seed swap where gardeners can trade heirloom seeds from their own collections.

Midsummer Night Market

On the third Saturday of July the market extends its hours for an evening event from 5pm to 9pm. Vendors illuminate their stalls with lanterns and string lights, and the town square transforms into an outdoor dining space. Local restaurants partner with market vendors to create special dishes using fresh market produce. Live music continues throughout the evening.

Harvest Festival

The Harvest Festival is held on the last Saturday of October and is the most popular event of the market season. Attractions include a pumpkin and squash display contest judged by a panel of local chefs, apple cider pressing demonstrations, a chilli cook-off featuring market vendors, expanded live music, and a costume parade for children. The festival typically draws over two thousand visitors throughout the day.

Season Closing Day

The final market of the season is held on the last Saturday of November and includes a special thank-you event for all vendors, volunteers, and loyal market customers. Vendors offer end-of-season prices on remaining stock, and the market board announces the schedule for the following year. Hot cider and local cheese are offered complimentary to all visitors.

Special Programmes

SNAP and Fresh Bucks

Greenfield Farmers Market accepts SNAP and EBT payments at the market information booth every Saturday. Participants receive a dollar-for-dollar match through the Fresh Bucks programme, up to twenty dollars per market visit. Fresh Bucks tokens can be used at any participating vendor stall displaying the Fresh Bucks sign. This programme is funded through a partnership with the county health department and a private foundation grant.

Vendor Mentorship Programme

New and aspiring market vendors can apply to the Vendor Mentorship Programme, which pairs new applicants with an experienced market vendor for one full season. Mentors provide guidance on stall setup, pricing, customer relations, and market regulations. Applications for the following season are accepted each October.
